item.title: Iran/US Conflict: First American Casualties, Major Escalation Across Region
item.description: Four US Army Reserve soldiers killed in drone strike in Kuwait — first American casualties confirmed. Israel launching new waves of strikes on Iran. Funerals held for students and staff killed in US-Israeli strike on school in Minab, Iran. Iran threatens to "set fire" to ships in Strait of Hormuz. Gulf states weighing response after Iranian retaliatory strikes. Hezbollah attacks Israel, triggering fresh Israeli bombing and mass displacement in Lebanon. European allies bolstering Cyprus defenses after drones target RAF Akrotiri. Oil/gas prices surging, shares tumbling. US citizens stranded across Middle East. (Source: BBC News)

item.title: AI/ML: OpenAI Restructures Military Deal; TikTok Refuses E2E Encryption
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announces prohibition on using company systems to spy on Americans, following backlash over military partnership. TikTok tells BBC it won't implement end-to-end encryption for DMs, diverging from WhatsApp and Messenger, claiming the decision protects users. (Source: BBC News)

item.title: Global: Markets Rattled, US-Ecuador Drug Ops, China Two Sessions
item.description: Gas and oil prices soar as Middle East conflict intensifies. US and Ecuador launch joint military operation against drug trafficking. China's annual "Two Sessions" political meetings underway. Kristi Noem faces bipartisan criticism at Senate hearing over DHS leadership. Trump threatens trade halt with Spain over military base access. (Source: BBC News)

item.title: Iran: 6 US Troops Killed, Strait of Hormuz Threatened, Embassies Closed
item.description: U.S. Central Command confirmed 6 service members killed in action. Iran vowed to close the Strait of Hormuz — oil supertanker rates hit all-time highs. U.S. closed embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait. Trump ordered Navy to escort tankers through the Strait and provide risk insurance for Gulf maritime trade. (Sources: CNBC)
